evals.prompt: Review an OSS-WS repository that contains CHANGELOG.md but no changelog.md. What should change?
evals.expected_output: The agent requires root changelog.md as the canonical OSS-WS changelog file and rejects CHANGELOG.md as canonical.
expectations: Requires changelog.md at the repository root
Requires the first heading to be # Changelog
Does not accept CHANGELOG.md as the canonical OSS-WS filename

evals.prompt: Rewrite these changelog bullets: 'feat: add templates', 'fixed typo', 'PR #12'.
evals.expected_output: Human-readable imperative changelog bullets that are self-contained and avoid raw commit or pull request title style.
expectations: Starts each item with an imperative present-tense verb
Makes each item understandable without the group heading
Avoids conventional-commit prefixes
Does not copy pull request titles without curation

evals.prompt: A phase added skill.md, rules, evals, and OpenAI agent metadata. Verify whether the phase is complete.
evals.expected_output: The agent checks that root changelog.md was updated, intended files are staged together, and the phase can be committed as one coherent unit.
expectations: Requires changelog.md to reflect the phase
Checks related skill, rule, eval, and agent files for consistency
Treats commit creation as part of completing the phase when available

evals.prompt: Review this commit subject for OSS-WS compliance: 'Added commit docs and fixed random typo'.
evals.expected_output: The agent rejects the subject because it is not imperative and mixes unrelated work, then suggests a scoped imperative subject.
expectations: Requires imperative present-tense subject wording
Flags unrelated work mixed into one commit
Suggests a scoped subject such as Add commit guide

evals.prompt: Draft a commit message for adding specs/oss-commit.md, docs/commit-guide.md, commit rules, commit evals, and a changelog update.
evals.expected_output: A coherent commit message with an imperative subject and optional body explaining that the files belong to one commit-standard phase.
expectations: Uses an imperative subject
Treats specs, docs, rules, evals, and changelog as one coherent standard phase
Adds body context if useful
Does not use raw chore or feat prefix as the primary meaning

evals.prompt: A commit changes a public template schema in a breaking way. Review the commit message.
evals.expected_output: The agent requires the breaking nature to be visible in the subject or first body paragraph and expects migration context when needed.
expectations: Marks breaking changes visibly
Explains required user action in the body when needed
Keeps the commit message human-readable
